articles.title: WHAT'S CHATGPT SAYING BEHIND YOUR BACK?
articles.summary: You're using ChatGPT and other AI tools to research products. So are your prospects. But do you know:

articles.title: Don't sleep on the next big discovery channel. Start free
WHY ADVERTISERS CAN'T OVERLOOK THE BUYING POWER OF VETERANS
articles.summary: Veterans are a high-income, tech-enabled audience with large purchasing power across home improvement, electronics, and digital shopping. With an average household income of $115K and above-average engagement with live and cable TV, they represent a premium consumer segment. Their strong presence in small business ownership and work-from-home roles creates further relevance for categories like office tech, software, and pet care.

articles.title: WHY DO THE YOUNG TRUST BRANDS MORE THAN THE OLD?
articles.summary: Research shows that younger people report trusting brands more than older people. The difference reflects how each group defines trust. Over-35s tend to trust charities, financial services, and healthcare providers, valuing ethics and safety. Meanwhile, under-35s trust everyday retailers and online services for reliability and performance. For older consumers, trust is a filter that must be earned through values and customer care. For younger consumers, trust is a baseline, and marketing should demonstrate consistent service and reliability.

articles.title: MORNING BREW'S GROWTH STRATEGY: HOW A NEWSLETTER BUILT A $75M MEDIA EMPIRE
articles.summary: Morning Brew turned business news into a media empire by combining an accessible voice, a viral referral program, and effective native advertising. Its newsletter, launched by two University of Michigan students in 2015, now reaches over 4M subscribers and drives 30% of new signups through referrals at almost no cost. The brand's conversational writing style and humor make complex topics easy to understand. Its multi-platform ecosystem across newsletters, podcasts, and social media built an audience and revenue model that has become a blueprint for modern media growth.

articles.title: HOW THIS CEO TURNED 25,000 HOURS OF SALES CALLS INTO A SELF-LEARNING GTM ENGINE (43 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: Matt Britton, CEO of Suzy, built a GTM system from 25,000 hours of customer calls. Using Zapier and Gong, he created automated workflows that generate summaries, sentiment scores, sales coaching, and keyword extraction from transcripts. The framework fuels paid media and SEO efforts without manual input. It also produces privacy-compliant content and live Slack alerts. His approach redefines the executive role in AI implementation by replacing delegation with direct capability building.

articles.title: GIVE YOUR MARKETING TEAM SUPERPOWERS WITH AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Lindy AI CMO [10] is a suite of powerful agents that ship beautiful marketing campaigns—fast. Simply enter your business's website, and watch AI agents study competitors, create messaging docs and campaign briefs, and generate creative assets. Everything is automatically organized in Airtable, ready to deploy.

articles.title: Start for free today and get $20 extra credits →
STAPE GTM HELPER
articles.summary: Stape GTM Helper is a free Google Tag Manager Chrome extension designed to speed up and simplify GTM debugging. It helps identify errors faster in GTM debug mode by providing a structured, color-coded view of GTM tags.

articles.title: WHAT WE LEARNED FROM 180 TOP-RANKED GOOGLE ADS
articles.summary: High-ranking Google Ads use urgency and power words like “today”, “now”, “free”, and “get” to grab attention. Numbers and superlatives such as “top” boost credibility and specificity, while adjectives like “luxury” and “expert” emphasize quality over price. Clear calls to action, most often phone calls, guide users toward immediate next steps. Headlines favor professional punctuation and title case, while dynamic keyword insertion is rarely used.

articles.title: WHAT TRIGGERS AI OVERVIEWS? 86 FACTORS AND 146 MILLION SERPS ANALYZED
articles.summary: AI Overviews appear on 21% of all keywords, showing that Google surfaces them most often for informational, question-based, and longer queries. 99.9% of AIOs appear on informational searches, 57.9% on question queries, and 46% on queries with seven or more words. AIOs are 1.9x more common for non-branded searches and frequently appear in categories like Science (43.6%) and Health (43%), while being rare in Shopping (3.2%) and News (15.1%).

articles.title: HOW THE BEST PRODUCTS REMOVE GUILT
articles.summary: Grüns grew fast by turning a chore into a treat. Founder Chad Janis saw that people liked supplements but hated messy powders. He made vitamins into gummies instead. The idea worked because it flipped guilt into permission. Eating candy became taking vitamins. Grüns showed that good products make people feel good about what they already want to do. The same rule applies to AI and other tools. Turn hard or guilty tasks into things people enjoy.

articles.title: HOW DO YOU MEASURE THE ROI OF TIMELESSNESS?
articles.summary: Timelessness is a brand's ability to stay relevant as trends change. It signals consistency and endurance seen in brands like Apple and Chanel. Timeless brands grow through trust, design, consistency, and resilience. Trust builds loyalty and profit, while design creates recognition and emotional connection. Consistency drives efficiency, and resilience protects pricing and market share. Measuring timelessness means tracking retention and execution efficiency. The payoff comes from patience and disciplined brand building that compounds over time.

articles.title: IN COMMON WITH: LIGHT ON/OFF TOGGLE
articles.summary: A neat design feature on In Common With's PLP lets you see how each lamp looks when turned on and off.
